forecast-period.text: A cold front over eastern districts will move to the east of SA overnight. The low pressure system associated with this front is expected to move slowly eastwards over waters to the south of the state and will extend a trough over southern parts of SA later Sunday and Monday. The low will then move over Bass Strait later Monday. A ridge will extend across SA from the west on Tuesday and a cold front will move across the south later Wednesday.
Wind and wave forecasts are averages. Wind gusts can be 40 per cent stronger than the forecast, and stronger still in squalls and thunderstorms. Maximum waves can be twice the forecast height.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones. Marine forecasts are also available on VHF and HF radio. View the radio schedule on www.bom.gov.au/marine
Check the latest Coastal Waters Forecasts for information on wind, wave and weather conditions for neighbouring coastal zones.

area.description: Far West Coast: WA-SA Border to Fowlers Bay

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Saturday for Far West Coast
forecast-period.text: West to southwesterly 20 to 25 knots, reaching up to 30 knots in the late afternoon.
2 to 2.5 metres.
Southwesterly 2 to 3 metres, increasing to 2.5 to 4 metres later in the evening.
Cloudy. 90% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm offshore.
Westerly 20 to 25 knots turning southwesterly in the early afternoon.
1.5 to 2 metres.
South to southwesterly 3 to 4 metres.
Partly cloudy. 95% chance of showers.
Southwesterly 15 to 20 knots turning southerly 10 to 15 knots in the late afternoon.
1 to 2 metres, decreasing to 1 metre during the afternoon.
South to southwesterly 2.5 to 3 metres.
Below 0.5 metres, tending southerly 1 to 1.5 metres during the morning, then increasing to 1 to 2 metres later in the evening.
Cloudy. 60% chance of showers.
Southerly 10 to 15 knots decreasing to about 10 knots during the morning.
Below 1 metre.
Southwesterly 1.5 to 2.5 metres.
Southerly 1.5 metres, decreasing to around 1 metre during the evening.
Partly cloudy.

area.description: Upper West Coast: Fowlers Bay to Elliston

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Saturday for Upper West Coast
forecast-period.text: Westerly 20 to 25 knots, reaching up to 30 knots in the late afternoon.
2 to 2.5 metres.
Southwesterly 2 to 3 metres, increasing to 3 to 4 metres south of Streaky Bay later in the evening.
Cloudy. 80% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm offshore.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Sunday for Upper West Coast
forecast-period.text: Westerly 20 to 25 knots, reaching up to 30 knots during the morning and early afternoon. Winds turning southwesterly 15 to 20 knots in the evening.
1.5 to 2.5 metres.
Southwesterly 4 to 5 metres.
Partly cloudy. 95% chance of showers.
Southwesterly 15 to 20 knots turning southerly 10 to 15 knots in the evening.
1 to 1.5 metres, decreasing to 1 metre during the afternoon.
Southwesterly 3 to 4 metres, decreasing to 3 metres around midday.
Partly cloudy. 70% chance of showers.
Southerly 10 to 15 knots decreasing to below 10 knots during the evening.
Below 1 metre.
Southwesterly 2 to 3 metres, decreasing to 2 metres during the evening.
Southerly 1 to 1.5 metres, decreasing to around 1 metre during the evening.
Cloudy.

area.description: Lower West Coast: Elliston to Cape Carnot

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Saturday for Lower West Coast
forecast-period.text: West to northwesterly 25 to 30 knots becoming westerly 20 to 25 knots in the evening then increasing to 20 to 30 knots in the late evening.
2 to 3 metres.
Westerly 2.5 to 3 metres, increasing to 2.5 to 4 metres by early evening, then tending west to southwesterly 4 to 5 metres later in the evening.
Cloudy. 80% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Sunday for Lower West Coast
forecast-period.text: Westerly 20 to 30 knots turning southwesterly 20 to 25 knots in the evening.
2 to 3 metres.
Southwesterly 4 to 5 metres.
Cloudy. 95% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm in the morning.
Southwesterly 15 to 25 knots decreasing to 10 to 15 knots in the evening.
1.5 to 2 metres, decreasing to 1 to 1.5 metres around midday.
Southwesterly 3 to 4 metres, decreasing to 3 metres around midday.
Cloudy. 80% chance of showers.
South to southwesterly 10 to 15 knots decreasing to below 10 knots during the afternoon.
Around 1 metre.
Southwesterly 2 to 3 metres.
Southeast to southwesterly around 1 metre.
Cloudy.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Saturday for Central Coast
forecast-period.text: Northwesterly 25 to 30 knots.
2.5 to 3 metres.
Westerly 3 to 5 metres.
Cloudy. 80% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Sunday for Central Coast
forecast-period.text: West to northwesterly 25 to 30 knots tending west to southwesterly during the day.
2.5 to 3 metres.
Westerly 4 to 6 metres, tending southwesterly 4 to 6 metres during the morning.
Cloudy. Near 100% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm.
West to southwesterly 25 to 30 knots becoming southwesterly 15 to 25 knots before dawn.
1.5 to 2.5 metres, decreasing to 1.5 metres during the afternoon.
Southwesterly 3 to 4 metres, decreasing to 3 metres later in the evening.
Partly cloudy. 90% chance of showers.
South to southwesterly 10 to 15 knots becoming west to southwesterly below 10 knots during the afternoon.
Around 1 metre, decreasing below 0.5 metres during the evening.
Southwesterly 2.5 to 3 metres.
Southerly below 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 1.5 metres during the afternoon.
Cloudy.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Gale Warning for Saturday for South Central Coast
forecast-period.text: Northwesterly 20 to 30 knots, reaching up to 35 knots west of Kangaroo Island in the late evening.
2 to 3 metres, increasing to 2.5 to 4 metres west of Kangaroo Island.
Southwesterly 2 to 3 metres, tending westerly 3 to 5 metres later in the evening.
Partly cloudy. 70% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Gale Warning for Sunday for South Central Coast
forecast-period.text: Northwesterly 25 to 35 knots decreasing to 25 to 30 knots before dawn then tending west to northwesterly 25 to 35 knots in the middle of the day.
2 to 3 metres.
Westerly 4 to 6 metres.
Cloudy. Near 100% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm.
Westerly 25 to 30 knots tending southwesterly 20 to 30 knots before dawn then decreasing to 15 to 25 knots in the early afternoon.
2 to 3 metres, decreasing to 1.5 to 2 metres by early evening.
Southwesterly 4 metres, decreasing to 3 metres later in the evening.
Partly cloudy. 90% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm in the early morning.
Southwesterly 15 to 20 knots decreasing to about 10 knots during the morning then tending westerly 10 to 15 knots during the evening.
1 to 1.5 metres, decreasing below 1 metre during the morning.
Southwesterly 2.5 to 3 metres.
Cloudy.

area.description: Upper South East Coast: Murray Mouth to Cape Jaffa

forecast-period.text: Northwesterly 15 to 20 knots, reaching up to 25 knots offshore in the late evening.
1 to 1.5 metres, increasing to 1 to 2 metres south of Salt Creek.
Southwesterly 1.5 to 2 metres.
Partly cloudy. 70% chance of showers.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Sunday for Upper South East Coast
forecast-period.text: Northwesterly 20 to 30 knots.
1 to 2 metres, increasing to 2 to 3 metres during the morning.
Westerly 2 to 3 metres inshore, increasing to 2 to 4 metres offshore during the morning.
Cloudy. 95% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm.
West to northwesterly 25 to 30 knots turning southwesterly 15 to 25 knots during the morning and early afternoon.
2 to 3 metres.
Westerly 2.5 to 3 metres, tending southwesterly 3 to 4 metres during the morning.
Cloudy. 95% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm in the morning.
Southwesterly 15 to 20 knots becoming westerly below 10 knots during the evening.
1 to 1.5 metres, decreasing below 1 metre during the morning.
Southwesterly 2.5 to 3 metres.
Cloudy.

area.description: Lower South East Coast: Cape Jaffa to SA-VIC Border

forecast-period.text: Northerly 15 to 25 knots.
1.5 to 2 metres.
Southwesterly 2 metres.
Cloudy. 90% chance of rain. The chance of a thunderstorm south of Port MacDonnell.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Sunday for Lower South East Coast
forecast-period.text: North to northwesterly 20 to 30 knots.
2 to 3 metres, increasing to 3 to 5 metres south of Beachport around midday.
West to northwesterly 2 to 3 metres inshore, increasing to 3 to 5 metres offshore during the morning.
Cloudy. 95% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm.
North to northwesterly 25 to 30 knots shifting southwesterly 20 to 30 knots during the morning.
3 to 4 metres, decreasing to 2 to 3 metres during the morning.
Westerly 2.5 to 4 metres, tending southwesterly 3 to 4 metres around midday.
Cloudy. Near 100% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm in the morning and afternoon.
Southwesterly 15 to 25 knots turning westerly 10 to 15 knots during the afternoon.
1 to 2 metres, decreasing below 1 metre during the afternoon.
Southwesterly 2.5 to 3 metres.
Cloudy. 50% chance of showers.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Saturday for Spencer Gulf
forecast-period.text: Northwesterly 15 to 20 knots, reaching up to 30 knots south of Cowell to Wallaroo in the late evening.
1 to 1.5 metres, increasing to 1.5 to 2 metres south of Cowell to Wallaroo.
South to southwesterly around 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 1.5 metres near the Yorke Peninsula coast by early evening.
Partly cloudy. 50% chance of showers south of Cowell to Wallaroo, near zero chance elsewhere.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Sunday for Spencer Gulf
forecast-period.text: Northwesterly 20 to 25 knots, reaching up to 30 knots south of Cowell to Wallaroo during the day. Winds turning westerly in the early afternoon.
2 to 3 metres.
South to southwesterly below 1 metre, increasing to 1.5 to 2.5 metres during the morning.
Partly cloudy. 95% chance of showers south of Port Neill to Port Victoria, 50% chance elsewhere.
Westerly 20 to 30 knots decreasing to 15 to 25 knots before dawn then tending southwesterly 15 to 20 knots in the middle of the day.
1.5 to 2.5 metres.
South to southwesterly around 1 metre, increasing to 1.5 to 2.5 metres south of Cowell to Wallaroo.
Partly cloudy. 80% chance of showers.
South to southwesterly 10 to 15 knots becoming variable below 10 knots during the evening.
Around 1 metre.
South to southwesterly around 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 2 metres south of Cowell to Wallaroo.
Cloudy.
Northwesterly 15 to 20 knots.
1 to 1.5 metres.
South to southwesterly below 1 metre.
Partly cloudy.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Sunday for Gulf St Vincent
forecast-period.text: Northwesterly 20 to 25 knots increasing to 20 to 30 knots in the early morning then decreasing to 20 to 25 knots in the late evening.
1.5 to 2.5 metres.

1.5 to 2.5 metres.
South to southwesterly below 1 metre.
Partly cloudy. 95% chance of showers.
Southwesterly 10 to 15 knots becoming variable below 10 knots during the evening.
Around 1 metre.
West to southwesterly below 0.5 metres.
Partly cloudy.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Saturday for Investigator Strait
forecast-period.text: Northwesterly 20 to 30 knots.
1.5 to 2.5 metres.
West to southwesterly below 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 2 metres by early evening, then tending westerly 2 to 3 metres later in the evening.
Partly cloudy. 60% chance of showers.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Sunday for Investigator Strait
forecast-period.text: Northwesterly 25 to 30 knots turning westerly in the evening.
2 to 3 metres.
Westerly 2 to 3 metres, increasing to 2 to 4 metres during the morning, then tending west to southwesterly 2 to 3 metres later in the evening.
Cloudy. 95% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm.
Westerly 20 to 30 knots turning southwesterly 15 to 20 knots during the morning.
1.5 to 2.5 metres, decreasing to 1 to 1.5 metres around midday.

Southwesterly 10 to 15 knots decreasing to below 10 knots during the day.
Around 1 metre.
West to southwesterly around 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 1.5 metres through the Backstairs Passage.
Partly cloudy.
